description: KNOWN GAP (pinned for visibility, per the R12 rule; fix is a later unit): an out-of-range citation marker is dropped from the citations list but its literal [n] text survives in the prose. Provenance: live CLI session 2026-06-11, plan 2026-06-11-001. Construction: document_chunks loads 3 chunks; the pre-fetch (P-201 exact-id resolution) registers them as [1]..[3]. The single scripted completion cites [2] (valid) and [9] (beyond the 3-slot registry) inline and in the block. TODAY parse_response resolves [2], logs a warning for [9] and drops it from the citations list -- but leaves the literal "[9]" marker in the delivered prose, a dangling reference pointing at nothing. The channel footer shows only the one resolved source. The future fix addresses the dangling-marker presentation; flipping this fixture's expected block is part of that fix.
user_messages: What routine maintenance does P-201 need?

document_chunks.content: Inspect the impeller for cavitation damage every quarter.
Check coupling alignment annually or after any seal work.

turns.content:
Inspect the impeller for cavitation damage every quarter [2]. The vendor datasheet also recommends an annual instrument recalibration [9].

<citations>
[2]
[9]
</citations>
